channel.title: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: Taliban Use Dress Code to Justify Arbitrary Arrests of Women
item.description: One morning in June, Halima (name changed), went to the market in Herat, her hometown in western Afghanistan, with her mother. She was wearing a long coat and a surgical mask covering her face. She could not have imagined that just a few minutes later she would be sitting in prison. Vehicles from the Ministry […]

item.title: More than 500 people feared dead in double Myanmar shipwreck tragedy
item.description: There is growing concern for at least 500 people believed to have been aboard two boats that reportedly capsized off Myanmar’s coast in recent days.

item.title: Countries vow faster action on development financing in annual declaration
item.description: UN Member States reaffirmed their commitment to the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) by adopting a declaration on Wednesday that they will implement reforms to help close the $4 trillion SDG financing gap.

item.title: ‘Heat deaths are not inevitable’: WHO pushes Europe to heat‑proof its hospitals
item.description: As Europe battles a deadly record-breaking heatwave, the World Health Organization (WHO) issued new guidance on Thursday to help countries face scorching temperatures now and in the future.

item.title: Peacekeepers step up support as families return to south Lebanon
item.description: With violence in south Lebanon significantly decreased since mid-June, UN peacekeepers there have returned to core functions such as protecting humanitarian aid and restoring critical infrastructure as families continue to return home.

item.title: Gaza farmers battle to rebuild as WFP warns of West Bank settler violence
item.description: The UN is supporting farmers in Gaza who are struggling to rebuild their smallholdings in areas devastated by the Hamas-Israel war.

item.title: WHO warns DR Congo Ebola outbreak is outpacing response as Uganda reaches milestone
item.description: The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) has become the third largest on record and is spreading faster than any previous one during the first month of exposure, the head of the World Health Organization (WHO) warned on Thursday.

item.title: Autonomous Weapons: The Wave of the Future in Military Conflicts Worldwide
item.description: As the international community continues to weigh the good, the bad and the deadly in artificial intelligence (AI), which is spreading far and wide with apparently no guardrails, the United Nations is taking a closer look at the impact, both positive and negative, of AI. UN Secretary General Antonio Guterres said last week that “the […]

item.title: Full Effects of Strait of Hormuz Disruption May Not Be Felt Until Second Half of 2026
item.description: The full economic impact of the disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz may not become clear until the second half of 2026, warns the United Nations Conference on Trade and Development (UNCTAD). Prior to the closure, an average of 129 maritime vessels transited daily through the strait, carrying approximately 34 percent of globally traded crude […]
